title: Dryopteris singalanensis
taxonomy: Dryopteris singalanensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns or buckler ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are usually terrestrial and have simple, undivided fronds. The classification of Dryopteris singalanensis is further supported by its characteristics, such as its leaf morphology and reproductive structures. This species is typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, including Southeast Asia.
uses: Dryopteris singalanensis has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. It is reportedly used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. The plant's leaves are often used in herbal remedies, and its extract is said to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge and may vary depending on the region and cultural context.

intro: Dryopteris singalanensis, also known as the Singalan fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris, characterized by its delicate fronds and adaptable growth habits. Traditionally, the Singalan fern has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, inflammation, and oxidative stress, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of the Singalan fern include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Singalan fern include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
